kcat_per_s.comment: kcat for recombinant A. flavus uricase (rasburicase scaffold). The canonical operating range across published preparations is ~8-20 s^-1 per active site; central ~13 s^-1. PASylated and PEGylated variants modulate this. Brenda entry EC 1.7.3.3 cluster.

Km_urate_M.comment: Km for urate, range 15-50 µM across A. flavus and related fungal uricases. Operates near or above saturation in tophi-rich joint environments (urate locally millimolar at MSU surface).

kcat_per_s.comment: Catalase is among the fastest known enzymes; turnover ~10^7-10^8 H2O2/s per heme/subunit. Standard textbook value ~4 x 10^7 s^-1 for bovine liver catalase (BLC) under physiologic O2 / pH 7 conditions. Tetramer has 4 active sites.

Km_h2o2_M.comment: Reported Km is high (effectively unsaturable at synovial-relevant µM concentrations); range 25 mM - 1.5 M depending on assay. Catalase operates strictly in the linear (first-order) regime for [H2O2] << Km — k_eff = kcat/Km × [E]. Some authors report apparent Km ~25-100 mM for the first-order step.

kcat_over_km_per_M_per_s.comment: Specificity constant for catalase at low [H2O2]. Range comes from kcat/Km with kcat ~10^7-10^8 and Km ~0.025-1 M. Central estimate ~4 x 10^7 M^-1 s^-1 (Chance / standard catalase literature).

stoichiometry.uricase_reaction: urate + O2 + H2O -> 5-hydroxyisourate + H2O2 -> allantoin + CO2 + H2O2

liu_2025_pebr_measured.comment: Liu 2025 J Nanobiotechnology PMID 41390400 measured specific activities. NOTE: 1 U URI conventionally = 1 µmol urate/min; 1 U catalase conventionally = 1 µmol H2O2 decomposed/min. So the immobilized CAT specific activity of 0.58 U/mg corresponds to only ~0.58 µmol H2O2/min/mg, which is roughly 10^4-fold below the bovine-liver-catalase canonical specific activity (~10^4 U/mg). This is the well-known commercial-catalase assay convention (sometimes 1 U = 1 mmol H2O2/min or other definitions) — without the explicit unit definition in the paper, treat as a relative comparison only and use the canonical catalase kcat for the diffusion model.
liu_2025_pebr_measured.primary_source: Liu et al, J Nanobiotechnology 2025;24(1):51, PMID 41390400, DOI 10.1186/s12951-025-03901-1
